rulings.Case Title: MAXIMILIANO RAZO et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Dana Companies, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID ROBERTS VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. On the issue of duty, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention that defendant owed decedent a duty. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of negating the duty element of plaintiffs¿ claims. However even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Dr. Cohen¿s declaration would create a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to decedent. As to the borrowed servant issue, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that its employees were special employees of the refinery/refineries at issue such that plaintiffs¿ exclusive remedy lies in the workers¿ compensation scheme. The Court did not consider defendant¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication due to defendant¿s violation of the requirements set forth in CRC 3.1350(b), (d) and plaintiffs¿ objections thereto. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT GLABB VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Fraser¿s Boiler Service, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (negligence and premises owner/contractor liability) and off calendar as to issues 2 and 3 (products liability and punitive damages). Assuming without deciding that defendant properly showed that it was not in control of any of the shipyards at issue, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that such lack of control is fatal to plaintiff¿s claims for negligence and premises owner/contractor liability. Plaintiff dismissed his products liability causes of action and claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN W MILLER et al VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Westburne Supply, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Miller was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention of liability against defendant. Defendant¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is off calendar as moot as it is undisputed that plaintiffs are not making a claim for punitive damages against defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
